codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the expected behavior. BetaPosterior for Bayesian confidence scoring, Procedure struct with effectiveness tracking, and SqliteProceduralStore with context similarity search are all implemented. 43 tests pass. Clippy clean.

issues.description: Dead code: `let _mean = self.mean();` computes mean but never uses it in confidence_score(). The underscore prefix marks it intentionally unused, but it's unclear if this was leftover debug code or intentionally no-op.

Test comment inconsistency: `test_procedure_expected_success_rate` comment says '3 successes, 1 failure => expected rate ≈ 3/5 = 0.6' but the test code has 4 record_outcome calls (3 true + 1 false). The actual rate is 3/4 = 0.75 which gives mean of 4/6 ≈ 0.667. The tolerance of 0.1 is wide enough to pass, but the comment is misleading.

sharedStateObservations.observation: ProceduralStore trait and SqliteProceduralStore are standalone, not integrated with SqliteMemoryStore or other memory components. This appears intentional as foundational infrastructure for future integration.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ProceduralStore only appears in procedural.rs and lib.rs exports. No usage in other memory modules.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed Rust skill conventions: async_trait for async traits, thiserror-compatible error handling via SwellError, proper Result return types, and tokio::test for async tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows skill invocation at start, all code follows patterns documented in Rust skill.

summary: Feature 'mem-procedural-memory' passes review. All three expected behaviors are implemented: procedures with effectiveness scores (BetaPosterior), Bayesian belief distribution tracking, and context similarity retrieval. 43 unit tests cover Beta distribution math, Procedure CRUD, and SQLite persistence. Two minor non-blocking issues: dead code in confidence_score() and a misleading test comment. No integration with existing memory stores which appears intentional for foundational work.
